title: Cell Renderers & Editors
intro: GraviGrid ships seven default cell renderers and five pre-registered cell editors so column definitions stay declarative — tags, deltas, money, dates, and in-cell editing without bespoke components. Reach for a renderer only when a cell needs real markup; plain formatting belongs in valueFormatter.

blocks.body:
These are the building blocks for GraviGrid columns: renderers (`TextCell`, `NumberCell`, `SingleDateCell`, `TagCell`, `DifferenceCell`, `NetValueCell`, `PriceDifferenceCell`) draw cell content, editors (`GraviSelectEditor`, `GraviDatePicker`, `GraviNumberEditor`, `GraviSwitchEditor`, `GraviCustValSelectEditor`) handle in-cell input, and validators (`validateNotEmptyString`, `validateInt`, `validateFloat`) guard `valueSetter`.

The decision rule: a `cellRenderer` mounts a React component in every cell — that is a real scroll-performance cost at grid scale. Use a renderer only when the cell needs markup (a tag, an icon, conditional color). Simple text, number, and date formatting goes in `valueFormatter`, which returns a string and mounts nothing.

Renderers import from `@gravitate-js/excalibrr` and are passed as component references in `cellRenderer`. Editors are already registered inside GraviGrid under string keys — reference them by name in `cellEditor`, no import needed.

blocks.title: TextCell
blocks.caption: TextCell wrapping string values in Texto — note both rows render uppercase regardless of input casing; the transform is always on.

blocks.title: NumberCell
blocks.caption: NumberCell right-aligned with comma grouping. Default toFixed={0} rounds 2.4567 to 2 — the Price row shows why you always pass toFixed on prices.

blocks.title: SingleDateCell
blocks.caption: SingleDateCell with no dateFormat prop — moment falls back to full ISO output (2024-01-15T00:00:00-08:00). Always pass dateFormat.

blocks.title: TagCell
blocks.caption: TagCell rendering neutral BBDTags for Status and Type. tagTheme props (success, error) recolor the tag; tipTitle adds a hover Tooltip.

blocks.title: The seven default renderers
blocks.intro: All export from @gravitate-js/excalibrr and read AG Grid cell params (value, data).

rows.name: TextCell
rows.when: Plain string display with Texto typography control (category, appearance, weight pass through). Returns null on empty value. Always uppercases — see gotchas.

rows.name: NumberCell
rows.when: Right-aligned numbers with comma grouping via addCommasToNumber. Pass toFixed for decimals, showNA to print N/A on empty.

rows.name: SingleDateCell
rows.when: Single date formatted through moment. dateFormat is required in practice — omit it and you get raw ISO.

rows.name: TagCell
rows.when: Status-style values as a BBDTag with optional icon and Tooltip. tagTheme spreads onto BBDTag, so { success: true } renders the green theme.
rows.code: cellRenderer: TagCell, cellRendererParams: { tagTheme: { success: true }, tipTitle: 'Status' }

rows.name: DifferenceCell
rows.when: A value plus a signed delta read from data[comparisonKey], rendered with caret up/down and success/error color. showCheck swaps a zero delta for a green check; upIsError forces the delta to error red regardless of sign; isPercent formats the main value as a percentage.
rows.code: cellRenderer: DifferenceCell, cellRendererParams: { comparisonKey: 'volume_delta', showCheck: true }

rows.name: NetValueCell
rows.when: Dollar amounts colored by sign — green positive, red negative, N/A when zero or empty. Renders a leading $ with addCommasToNumber(value, precision).

rows.name: PriceDifferenceCell
rows.when: Competitive price comparison: BBDTag is green when data.is_best, red otherwise, with the gap shown as (data.price_difference to 4 decimals) or (Lowest).

blocks.title: Renderer props that matter
blocks.intro: Verified against DefaultCellRenderers.tsx. TextCell, NumberCell, SingleDateCell, NetValueCell, and PriceDifferenceCell spread remaining props onto their underlying Texto or BBDTag; TagCell and DifferenceCell accept only their named props.

rows.notes: Decimal places via toLocaleString (min and max fraction digits) — values round, they do not truncate. Pass 4 for per-gallon prices, 2 for dollar totals.
Prints N/A when the value is empty. Beware zero: the renderer appends rather than replaces, so showNA on a 0 value renders 0N/A. Without it, empty cells render blank.
moment format string. Required in practice — undefined yields full ISO output.
Spread onto BBDTag — boolean theme props like { success: true } or { error: true }. Omit for the neutral tag.
Wraps the tag in an antd Tooltip. icon + iconClass render a leading icon inside the tag.
Field name on row data holding the delta — the cell reads data[comparisonKey], not value, for the caret and color.
Forces the delta to error red regardless of sign — the source returns 'error' before checking direction, so down-deltas go red too. Use when an increase is bad (cost, exceptions).
Decimal places on the $-prefixed amount. Zero and empty both render N/A.
Decimal places on the price. Row data must supply is_best (drives green/red); price_difference renders to a fixed 4 decimals, and a missing or zero price_difference renders (Lowest).

blocks.title: Pre-registered editors
blocks.intro: GraviGrid registers these under string keys in its components map — set cellEditor to the string, no import. All commit through AG Grid's getValue contract; every editor opens as a popup except GraviDatePicker, which declares no isPopup and edits inline.

rows.name: GraviSelectEditor
rows.when: Pick from a fixed list. Popup antd Select, opens immediately, supports multiple/tags modes. Not for booleans — see gotchas.

rows.name: GraviDatePicker
rows.when: Single date edits. Commits the moment a date is picked (calls stopEditing) and returns an ISO string — your valueFormatter or renderer must handle ISO input.

rows.name: GraviNumberEditor
rows.when: Numeric edits with rails. antd InputNumber popup honoring min, max, precision, step.

rows.name: GraviSwitchEditor
rows.when: Boolean fields. Centered antd Switch popup seeded from the current value.

rows.name: GraviCustValSelectEditor
rows.when: Multi-select where users can append new values (built for counterparty email lists — validates new entries as emails before Add enables).

blocks.title: Validators (valueSetter guards)
blocks.intro: Each writes params.data[field] itself on success and fires a NotificationMessage on failure — wire them directly as the column's valueSetter.

rows.notes: Rejects empty string with a 'Field required' notification; any other value commits.
parseInt with range checks — out-of-range and non-numeric input each get their own notification and the edit is rejected.
parseFloat with the same range/NaN guards. Use for prices and rates.

blocks.title: Canonical column definitions
blocks.note: columnDefs must be memoized — an unmemoized array recreates every render and resets grid state. Editors are referenced by registered string name; renderers and validators import from @gravitate-js/excalibrr.

items.do: Format plain text, numbers, and dates with valueFormatter
items.dont: Mount a cellRenderer for simple formatting
items.why: Every cellRenderer is a React mount per cell — at thousands of cells that is real scroll jank for zero visual gain.

items.do: Write money as decimal dollars — $0.0100/gal, precision 4 for per-gallon
items.dont: Use cents symbols or cent units anywhere in grid copy
items.why: Gravitate's standard is decimal dollars; NetValueCell and PriceDifferenceCell already prefix $.

items.do: Pair every editable column with a validator valueSetter and isBulkEditable: true
items.why: Without a valueSetter edits can silently fail, and bulk edit skips columns missing isBulkEditable.

items.do: Pass toFixed/precision explicitly on every numeric renderer
items.why: All numeric renderers default to 0 decimal places — a $2.4567 rack price renders as $2.

items.title: TextCell always uppercases
items.detail: The source ternary resolves to 'uppercase' in both branches, so the toUpperCase prop is dead — every TextCell renders uppercase. For mixed-case display use valueFormatter or a plain Texto renderer.

items.title: SingleDateCell without dateFormat dumps raw ISO
items.detail: moment(value).format(undefined) returns the full ISO string (2024-01-15T00:00:00-08:00). Always pass dateFormat, e.g. 'MM/DD/YYYY'.

items.title: GraviSelectEditor cannot commit yes/no options
items.detail: Its change handler maps 'yes'/'no' strings to booleans but skips the state update in those branches, so the picked value never persists. Booleans belong in GraviSwitchEditor.

items.title: GraviDatePicker commits instantly and returns ISO
items.detail: Picking a date calls api.stopEditing() immediately — there is no confirm step — and getValue returns moment(date).toISOString(). Format on display, not in the stored value.

items.title: DifferenceCell reads the delta from row data, not value
items.detail: The caret, sign, and color come from data[comparisonKey]; value is only the headline number. Forgetting to populate the comparison field renders a misleading bare value.

items.title: cellRenderer is a per-cell React mount
items.detail: This is the standing perf rule from the repo's mistakes table: simple text/number formatting must use valueFormatter. Reserve renderers for markup — tags, icons, conditional color.
